text.title: release-planning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for release-planning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Weekend on-call has reduced coverage and slower mean-time-to-restore. A Monday-Wednesday production window keeps the full team available for the critical 24-hour monitoring period.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every release must have a one-command rollback path that has been dry-run on staging. Without it, incident response time multiplies and pressure leads to poor decisions.

failure-mode.description: Last-minute feature additions after the freeze.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Late additions skip the readiness checklist and break the test baseline. Enforce a 24-48 hour code freeze before every release; later changes go to the next release.

failure-mode.description: Skipping communication because the release is "just a patch".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users and support staff notice changes regardless of size. Silent releases generate confused tickets. Always publish at least a CHANGELOG entry and an in-app changelog link.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Staging catches integration issues that unit tests miss. Mandatory for any release touching data migrations, third-party integrations, or infrastructure.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Error rate, latency, and conversion metrics can degrade subtly after a release. Watch for at least 24 hours before declaring success and updating the status page.

failure-mode.description: Monster releases bundling months of features.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Large releases have higher risk, harder rollbacks, and diluted communication impact. Smaller, more frequent releases reduce blast radius and keep users engaged with steady visible progress.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A premature announcement followed by a delay or rollback erodes trust faster than any feature can rebuild it. Verify smoke + 30 minutes of clean metrics before publishing the announcement.
